excerpt:
Rocky Mountain Area(PL 4)   
Fire Activity and Teams Assigned Totals 
New fires: 19 
New large incidents: 2 
Uncontained large fires: 10 
CIMTs Committed: 5 
 
Aspen Acres, Custer County. CIMT (NR Team 6). Twenty miles southwest of Pueblo, CO. Timber. Moderate 
fire behavior with flanking and single tree torching. Numerous residences and communication infrastructure 
threatened. Evacuations, area, road and trail closures in effect. 
 
Gold Mountain, Grand Mesa, Uncompahgre and Gunnison NF, USFS. CIMT (RM Team 2). Two miles north 
of Ouray, CO. Grass, brush and timber. Minimal fire behavior with flanking, backing and torching. Numerous 
structures and critical infrastructure threatened. Evacuations, area, road and trail closures in effect. 
Precipitation occurred over the fire area yesterday. 
 
Elk, Grand Mesa, Uncompahgre and Gunnison NF, USFS. CIMT (NW Team 8). Seven miles northwest of 
Lake City, CO. Timber, heavy slash and grass. Minimal fire behavior with flanking, backing and creeping. 
Residences threatened. Area, road and trail closures in effect. Precipitation occurred over the fire area 
yesterday. 
 
Willow, Pike and San Isabel NF, USFS. CIMT (CA Team 8). Six miles west of Leadville, CO. Brush, closed 
timber litter and timber. Moderate fire behavior with creeping, isolated torching and smoldering. Residences 
and critical infrastructure threatened. Evacuations, area, road and trail closures in effect. Precipitation occurred 
over the fire area yesterday. 
 
Fishhook, Medicine Bow-Routt NF, USFS. CIMT (SW Team 1). Eight miles southeast of Steamboat Springs, 
CO. Grass, brush and timber. Minimal fire behavior with creeping and smoldering. Energy infrastructure 
threatened. Area, road and trail closures in effect. Reduction in acreage due to more accurate mapping. 
Precipitation occurred over the fire area yesterday. 
 
Ferris, San Juan NF, USFS. Seven miles east of Cahone, CO. Timber and brush. Minimal fire behavior with 
smoldering. Area, road and trail closures in effect. 
 
* Keystone, White River Field Office, BLM. Forty-five miles northeast of Meeker, CO. Brush and timber. 
Moderate fire behavior with torching and creeping. Residences threatened. 
 
* Fuhr Gulch, Moffat County. Three miles southwest of Craig, CO. Brush and grass. Active fire behavior with 
running, torching and crowning.
